speaker: ANGELO
text: I am sorry, sir, that I have hindered you,

speaker: ANGELO
text: But I protest he had the chain of me,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Though most dishonestly he doth deny it.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: How is the man esteemed here in the city?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Of very reverend reputation, sir,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Of credit infinite, highly beloved,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Second to none that lives here in the city.

speaker: ANGELO
text: His word might bear my wealth at any time.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Speak softly. Yonder, as I think, he walks.

speaker: ANGELO
text: ’Tis so, and that self chain about his neck

speaker: ANGELO
text: Which he forswore most monstrously to have.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Good sir, draw near to me. I’ll speak to him.—

speaker: ANGELO
text: Signior Antipholus, I wonder much

speaker: ANGELO
text: That you would put me to this shame and trouble,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And not without some scandal to yourself,

speaker: ANGELO
text: With circumstance and oaths so to deny

speaker: ANGELO
text: This chain, which now you wear so openly.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Besides the charge, the shame, imprisonment,

speaker: ANGELO
text: You have done wrong to this my honest friend,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Who, but for staying on our controversy,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Had hoisted sail and put to sea today.

speaker: ANGELO
text: This chain you had of me. Can you deny it?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I think I had. I never did deny it.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Yes, that you did, sir, and forswore it too.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Who heard me to deny it or forswear it?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: These ears of mine, thou know’st, did hear thee.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Fie on thee, wretch. ’Tis pity that thou liv’st

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: To walk where any honest men resort.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thou art a villain to impeach me thus.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I’ll prove mine honor and mine honesty

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Against thee presently if thou dar’st stand.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: I dare, and do defy thee for a villain.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Hold, hurt him not, for God’s sake. He is mad.—

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Some get within him; take his sword away.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Bind Dromio too, and bear them to my house!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Run, master, run. For God’s sake, take a house.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: This is some priory. In, or we are spoiled.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Be quiet, people. Wherefore throng you hither?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: To fetch my poor distracted husband hence.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Let us come in, that we may bind him fast

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And bear him home for his recovery.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I knew he was not in his perfect wits.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: I am sorry now that I did draw on him.

speaker: ABBESS
text: How long hath this possession held the man?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: This week he hath been heavy, sour, sad,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And much different from the man he was.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: But till this afternoon his passion

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Ne’er brake into extremity of rage.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Hath he not lost much wealth by wrack of sea?

speaker: ABBESS
text: Buried some dear friend? Hath not else his eye

speaker: ABBESS
text: Strayed his affection in unlawful love,

speaker: ABBESS
text: A sin prevailing much in youthful men

speaker: ABBESS
text: Who give their eyes the liberty of gazing?

speaker: ABBESS
text: Which of these sorrows is he subject to?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: To none of these, except it be the last,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Namely, some love that drew him oft from home.

speaker: ABBESS
text: You should for that have reprehended him.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Why, so I did.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Ay, but not rough enough.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: As roughly as my modesty would let me.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Haply in private.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And in assemblies too.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Ay, but not enough.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: It was the copy of our conference.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: In bed he slept not for my urging it;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: At board he fed not for my urging it.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Alone, it was the subject of my theme;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: In company I often glancèd it.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Still did I tell him it was vile and bad.

speaker: ABBESS
text: And thereof came it that the man was mad.

speaker: ABBESS
text: The venom clamors of a jealous woman

speaker: ABBESS
text: Poisons more deadly than a mad dog’s tooth.

speaker: ABBESS
text: It seems his sleeps were hindered by thy railing,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And thereof comes it that his head is light.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Thou sayst his meat was sauced with thy upbraidings.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Unquiet meals make ill digestions.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Thereof the raging fire of fever bred,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And what’s a fever but a fit of madness?

speaker: ABBESS
text: Thou sayest his sports were hindered by thy brawls.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Sweet recreation barred, what doth ensue

speaker: ABBESS
text: But moody and dull melancholy,

speaker: ABBESS
text: Kinsman to grim and comfortless despair,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And at her heels a huge infectious troop

speaker: ABBESS
text: Of pale distemperatures and foes to life?

speaker: ABBESS
text: In food, in sport, and life-preserving rest

speaker: ABBESS
text: To be disturbed would mad or man or beast.

speaker: ABBESS
text: The consequence is, then, thy jealous fits

speaker: ABBESS
text: Hath scared thy husband from the use of wits.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: She never reprehended him but mildly

speaker: LUCIANA
text: When he demeaned himself rough, rude, and wildly.—

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Why bear you these rebukes and answer not?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: She did betray me to my own reproof.—

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Good people, enter and lay hold on him.

speaker: ABBESS
text: No, not a creature enters in my house.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Then let your servants bring my husband forth.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Neither. He took this place for sanctuary,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And it shall privilege him from your hands

speaker: ABBESS
text: Till I have brought him to his wits again

speaker: ABBESS
text: Or lose my labor in assaying it.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I will attend my husband, be his nurse,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Diet his sickness, for it is my office

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And will have no attorney but myself;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And therefore let me have him home with me.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Be patient, for I will not let him stir

speaker: ABBESS
text: Till I have used the approvèd means I have,

speaker: ABBESS
text: With wholesome syrups, drugs, and holy prayers,

speaker: ABBESS
text: To make of him a formal man again.

speaker: ABBESS
text: It is a branch and parcel of mine oath,

speaker: ABBESS
text: A charitable duty of my order.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Therefore depart and leave him here with me.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I will not hence and leave my husband here;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And ill it doth beseem your holiness

speaker: ADRIANA
text: To separate the husband and the wife.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Be quiet and depart. Thou shalt not have him.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Complain unto the Duke of this indignity.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Come, go. I will fall prostrate at his feet

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And never rise until my tears and prayers

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Have won his grace to come in person hither

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And take perforce my husband from the Abbess.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: By this, I think, the dial points at five.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Anon, I’m sure, the Duke himself in person

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Comes this way to the melancholy vale,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: The place of death and sorry execution

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Behind the ditches of the abbey here.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Upon what cause?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: To see a reverend Syracusian merchant,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Who put unluckily into this bay

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Against the laws and statutes of this town,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Beheaded publicly for his offense.

speaker: ANGELO
text: See where they come. We will behold his death.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Kneel to the Duke before he pass the abbey.

speaker: DUKE
text: Yet once again proclaim it publicly,

speaker: DUKE
text: If any friend will pay the sum for him,

speaker: DUKE
text: He shall not die; so much we tender him.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Justice, most sacred duke, against the Abbess.

speaker: DUKE
text: She is a virtuous and a reverend lady.

speaker: DUKE
text: It cannot be that she hath done thee wrong.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: May it please your Grace, Antipholus my husband,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Who I made lord of me and all I had

speaker: ADRIANA
text: At your important letters, this ill day

speaker: ADRIANA
text: A most outrageous fit of madness took him,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That desp’rately he hurried through the street,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: With him his bondman, all as mad as he,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Doing displeasure to the citizens

speaker: ADRIANA
text: By rushing in their houses, bearing thence

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Rings, jewels, anything his rage did like.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Once did I get him bound and sent him home

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Whilst to take order for the wrongs I went

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That here and there his fury had committed.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Anon, I wot not by what strong escape,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: He broke from those that had the guard of him,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And with his mad attendant and himself,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Each one with ireful passion, with drawn swords,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Met us again and, madly bent on us,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Chased us away, till raising of more aid,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: We came again to bind them. Then they fled

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Into this abbey, whither we pursued them,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And here the Abbess shuts the gates on us

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And will not suffer us to fetch him out,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Nor send him forth that we may bear him hence.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Therefore, most gracious duke, with thy command

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Let him be brought forth and borne hence for help.

speaker: DUKE
text: Long since, thy husband served me in my wars,

speaker: DUKE
text: And I to thee engaged a prince’s word,

speaker: DUKE
text: When thou didst make him master of thy bed,

speaker: DUKE
text: To do him all the grace and good I could.

speaker: DUKE
text: Go, some of you, knock at the abbey gate,

speaker: DUKE
text: And bid the Lady Abbess come to me.

speaker: DUKE
text: I will determine this before I stir.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: O mistress, mistress, shift and save yourself.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My master and his man are both broke loose,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Beaten the maids a-row, and bound the doctor,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Whose beard they have singed off with brands of fire,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And ever as it blazed they threw on him

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Great pails of puddled mire to quench the hair.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My master preaches patience to him, and the while

speaker: MESSENGER
text: His man with scissors nicks him like a fool;

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And sure, unless you send some present help,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Between them they will kill the conjurer.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Peace, fool. Thy master and his man are here,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And that is false thou dost report to us.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Mistress, upon my life I tell you true.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I have not breathed almost since I did see it.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: He cries for you and vows, if he can take you,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To scorch your face and to disfigure you.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Hark, hark, I hear him, mistress. Fly, begone!

speaker: DUKE
text: Come, stand by me. Fear nothing.—Guard with halberds.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Ay me, it is my husband. Witness you

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That he is borne about invisible.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Even now we housed him in the abbey here,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And now he’s there, past thought of human reason.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Justice, most gracious duke. O, grant me justice,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Even for the service that long since I did thee

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: When I bestrid thee in the wars and took

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Deep scars to save thy life. Even for the blood

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That then I lost for thee, now grant me justice.

speaker: EGEON
text: Unless the fear of death doth make me dote,

speaker: EGEON
text: I see my son Antipholus and Dromio.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Justice, sweet prince, against that woman there,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: She whom thou gav’st to me to be my wife,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That hath abusèd and dishonored me

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Even in the strength and height of injury.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Beyond imagination is the wrong

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That she this day hath shameless thrown on me.

speaker: DUKE
text: Discover how, and thou shalt find me just.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: This day, great duke, she shut the doors upon me

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: While she with harlots feasted in my house.

speaker: DUKE
text: A grievous fault.—Say, woman, didst thou so?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: No, my good lord. Myself, he, and my sister

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Today did dine together. So befall my soul

speaker: ADRIANA
text: As this is false he burdens me withal.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Ne’er may I look on day nor sleep on night

speaker: LUCIANA
text: But she tells to your Highness simple truth.

speaker: ANGELO
text: O perjured woman!—They are both forsworn.

speaker: ANGELO
text: In this the madman justly chargeth them.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: My liege, I am advisèd what I say,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Neither disturbed with the effect of wine,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Nor heady-rash provoked with raging ire,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Albeit my wrongs might make one wiser mad.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: This woman locked me out this day from dinner.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That goldsmith there, were he not packed with her,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Could witness it, for he was with me then,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Who parted with me to go fetch a chain,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Promising to bring it to the Porpentine,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Where Balthasar and I did dine together.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Our dinner done and he not coming thither,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I went to seek him. In the street I met him,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And in his company that gentleman.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: There did this perjured goldsmith swear me down

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That I this day of him received the chain,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Which, God He knows, I saw not; for the which

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: He did arrest me with an officer.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I did obey and sent my peasant home

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For certain ducats. He with none returned.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Then fairly I bespoke the officer

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To go in person with me to my house.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: By th’ way we met

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: My wife, her sister, and a rabble more

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Of vile confederates. Along with them

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: They brought one Pinch, a hungry, lean-faced villain,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: A mere anatomy, a mountebank,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: A threadbare juggler, and a fortune-teller,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: A needy, hollow-eyed, sharp-looking wretch,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: A living dead man. This pernicious slave,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Forsooth, took on him as a conjurer,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And, gazing in mine eyes, feeling my pulse,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And with no face (as ’twere) outfacing me,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Cries out I was possessed. Then all together

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: They fell upon me, bound me, bore me thence,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And in a dark and dankish vault at home

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: There left me and my man, both bound together,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Till gnawing with my teeth my bonds in sunder,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I gained my freedom and immediately

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Ran hither to your Grace, whom I beseech

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To give me ample satisfaction

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For these deep shames and great indignities.

speaker: ANGELO
text: My lord, in truth, thus far I witness with him:

speaker: ANGELO
text: That he dined not at home, but was locked out.

speaker: DUKE
text: But had he such a chain of thee or no?

speaker: ANGELO
text: He had, my lord, and when he ran in here,

speaker: ANGELO
text: These people saw the chain about his neck.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Besides, I will be sworn these ears of mine

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Heard you confess you had the chain of him

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: After you first forswore it on the mart,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: And thereupon I drew my sword on you,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: And then you fled into this abbey here,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: From whence I think you are come by miracle.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I never came within these abbey walls,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Nor ever didst thou draw thy sword on me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I never saw the chain, so help me heaven,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And this is false you burden me withal.

speaker: DUKE
text: Why, what an intricate impeach is this!

speaker: DUKE
text: I think you all have drunk of Circe’s cup.

speaker: DUKE
text: If here you housed him, here he would have been.

speaker: DUKE
text: If he were mad, he would not plead so coldly.

speaker: DUKE
text:
To
                 
                Adriana
                .
                 
              You say he dined at home; the goldsmith here

speaker: DUKE
text:
Denies that saying.
                 
                To
                 
                Dromio
                 
                of
                 
                Ephesus
                .
               Sirrah, what say you?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Sir, he dined with her there at the Porpentine.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: He did, and from my finger snatched that ring.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: ’Tis true, my liege, this ring I had of her.

speaker: DUKE
text: Saw’st thou him enter at the abbey here?

speaker: COURTESAN
text: As sure, my liege, as I do see your Grace.

speaker: DUKE
text: Why, this is strange.—Go call the Abbess hither.

speaker: DUKE
text: I think you are all mated or stark mad.

speaker: EGEON
text: Most mighty duke, vouchsafe me speak a word.

speaker: EGEON
text: Haply I see a friend will save my life

speaker: EGEON
text: And pay the sum that may deliver me.

speaker: DUKE
text: Speak freely, Syracusian, what thou wilt.

speaker: EGEON
text: Is not your name, sir, called Antipholus?

speaker: EGEON
text: And is not that your bondman Dromio?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Within this hour I was his bondman, sir,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But he, I thank him, gnawed in two my cords.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Now am I Dromio, and his man, unbound.

speaker: EGEON
text: I am sure you both of you remember me.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Ourselves we do remember, sir, by you,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For lately we were bound as you are now.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You are not Pinch’s patient, are you, sir?

speaker: EGEON
text: Why look you strange on me? You know me well.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I never saw you in my life till now.

speaker: EGEON
text: O, grief hath changed me since you saw me last,

speaker: EGEON
text: And careful hours with time’s deformèd hand

speaker: EGEON
text: Have written strange defeatures in my face.

speaker: EGEON
text: But tell me yet, dost thou not know my voice?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Neither.

speaker: EGEON
text: Dromio, nor thou?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: No, trust me, sir, nor I.

speaker: EGEON
text: I am sure thou dost.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Ay, sir, but I am sure I do not, and

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: whatsoever a man denies, you are now bound to

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: believe him.

speaker: EGEON
text: Not know my voice! O time’s extremity,

speaker: EGEON
text: Hast thou so cracked and splitted my poor tongue

speaker: EGEON
text: In seven short years that here my only son

speaker: EGEON
text: Knows not my feeble key of untuned cares?

speaker: EGEON
text: Though now this grainèd face of mine be hid

speaker: EGEON
text: In sap-consuming winter’s drizzled snow,

speaker: EGEON
text: And all the conduits of my blood froze up,

speaker: EGEON
text: Yet hath my night of life some memory,

speaker: EGEON
text: My wasting lamps some fading glimmer left,

speaker: EGEON
text: My dull deaf ears a little use to hear.

speaker: EGEON
text: All these old witnesses—I cannot err—

speaker: EGEON
text: Tell me thou art my son Antipholus.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I never saw my father in my life.

speaker: EGEON
text: But seven years since, in Syracusa, boy,

speaker: EGEON
text: Thou know’st we parted. But perhaps, my son,

speaker: EGEON
text: Thou sham’st to acknowledge me in misery.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: The Duke and all that know me in the city

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Can witness with me that it is not so.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I ne’er saw Syracusa in my life.

speaker: DUKE
text: I tell thee, Syracusian, twenty years

speaker: DUKE
text: Have I been patron to Antipholus,

speaker: DUKE
text: During which time he ne’er saw Syracusa.

speaker: DUKE
text: I see thy age and dangers make thee dote.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Most mighty duke, behold a man much wronged.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I see two husbands, or mine eyes deceive me.

speaker: DUKE
text: One of these men is genius to the other.

speaker: DUKE
text: And so, of these, which is the natural man

speaker: DUKE
text: And which the spirit? Who deciphers them?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I, sir, am Dromio. Command him away.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I, sir, am Dromio. Pray, let me stay.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Egeon art thou not, or else his ghost?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: O, my old master.—Who hath bound him here?

speaker: ABBESS
text: Whoever bound him, I will loose his bonds

speaker: ABBESS
text: And gain a husband by his liberty.—

speaker: ABBESS
text: Speak, old Egeon, if thou be’st the man

speaker: ABBESS
text: That hadst a wife once called Emilia,

speaker: ABBESS
text: That bore thee at a burden two fair sons.

speaker: ABBESS
text: O, if thou be’st the same Egeon, speak,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And speak unto the same Emilia.

speaker: DUKE
text: Why, here begins his morning story right:

speaker: DUKE
text: These two Antipholus’, these two so like,

speaker: DUKE
text: And these two Dromios, one in semblance—

speaker: DUKE
text: Besides her urging of her wrack at sea—

speaker: DUKE
text: These are the parents to these children,

speaker: DUKE
text: Which accidentally are met together.

speaker: EGEON
text: If I dream not, thou art Emilia.

speaker: EGEON
text: If thou art she, tell me, where is that son

speaker: EGEON
text: That floated with thee on the fatal raft?

speaker: ABBESS
text: By men of Epidamium he and I

speaker: ABBESS
text: And the twin Dromio all were taken up;

speaker: ABBESS
text: But by and by rude fishermen of Corinth

speaker: ABBESS
text: By force took Dromio and my son from them,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And me they left with those of Epidamium.

speaker: ABBESS
text: What then became of them I cannot tell;

speaker: ABBESS
text: I to this fortune that you see me in.

speaker: DUKE
text: Antipholus, thou cam’st from Corinth first.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: No, sir, not I. I came from Syracuse.

speaker: DUKE
text: Stay, stand apart. I know not which is which.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I came from Corinth, my most gracious lord.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And I with him.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Brought to this town by that most famous warrior

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Duke Menaphon, your most renownèd uncle.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Which of you two did dine with me today?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I, gentle mistress.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And are not you my husband?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: No, I say nay to that.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And so do I, yet did she call me so,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And this fair gentlewoman, her sister here,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text:
Did call me brother.
                 
                To
                 
                Luciana
                .
               What I told you then

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I hope I shall have leisure to make good,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If this be not a dream I see and hear.

speaker: ANGELO
text: That is the chain, sir, which you had of me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I think it be, sir. I deny it not.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And you, sir, for this chain arrested me.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I think I did, sir. I deny it not.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I sent you money, sir, to be your bail

speaker: ADRIANA
text: By Dromio, but I think he brought it not.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: No, none by me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: This purse of ducats I received from you,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And Dromio my man did bring them me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I see we still did meet each other’s man,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And I was ta’en for him, and he for me,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And thereupon these errors are arose.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: These ducats pawn I for my father here.

speaker: DUKE
text: It shall not need. Thy father hath his life.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Sir, I must have that diamond from you.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: There, take it, and much thanks for my good cheer.

speaker: ABBESS
text: Renownèd duke, vouchsafe to take the pains

speaker: ABBESS
text: To go with us into the abbey here

speaker: ABBESS
text: And hear at large discoursèd all our fortunes,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And all that are assembled in this place

speaker: ABBESS
text: That by this sympathizèd one day’s error

speaker: ABBESS
text: Have suffered wrong. Go, keep us company,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And we shall make full satisfaction.—

speaker: ABBESS
text: Thirty-three years have I but gone in travail

speaker: ABBESS
text: Of you, my sons, and till this present hour

speaker: ABBESS
text: My heavy burden ne’er deliverèd.—

speaker: ABBESS
text: The Duke, my husband, and my children both,

speaker: ABBESS
text: And you, the calendars of their nativity,

speaker: ABBESS
text: Go to a gossips’ feast, and go with me.

speaker: ABBESS
text: After so long grief, such nativity!

speaker: DUKE
text: With all my heart I’ll gossip at this feast.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Master, shall I fetch your stuff from shipboard?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Dromio, what stuff of mine hast thou embarked?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Your goods that lay at host, sir, in the Centaur.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: He speaks to me.—I am your master, Dromio.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Come, go with us. We’ll look to that anon.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Embrace thy brother there. Rejoice with him.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: There is a fat friend at your master’s house

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: That kitchened me for you today at dinner.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: She now shall be my sister, not my wife.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Methinks you are my glass, and not my brother.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I see by you I am a sweet-faced youth.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Will you walk in to see their gossiping?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Not I, sir. You are my elder.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That’s a question. How shall we

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: try it?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: We’ll draw cuts for the signior.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Till then, lead thou first.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Nay, then, thus:

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: We came into the world like brother and brother,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And now let’s go hand in hand, not one before another.
